Feedback? Ideas? Complaints? Suggestions? Request for report about other area? send me a message!
This page was generated using data obtained on various dates including 2023-01-05, 2023-01-06. Please, send me a message if you want it updated!




Remember to check whatever edit makes sense! All reports are at this page because this tasks require human judgment to verify whatever proposed edit makes sense.
  • wikipedia tag links to 404
  • link to an unlinkable article
  • wikipedia wikidata mismatch
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a transport accident
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a television series
  • invalid old-style wikipedia tag
  • malformed wikidata tag
  • malformed wikipedia tag
  • information board with wikipedia tag, not subject:wikipedia
  • information board with wikidata tag, not subject:wikidata
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to an object that exists outside physical reality
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a human
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to an animal or plant (and not an individual one)
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a vehicle model or class
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a brand
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a chain store
  • blacklisted connection with known replacement
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a historical event
  • wikipedia tag in outdated form and there is mismatch between links
  • wikipedia wikidata mismatch - wikipedia points to disambiguation page and wikidata does not
  • wikipedia/wikidata type tag that is incorrect according to not:* tag
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a social movement
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to an aspect in a geographic region
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a military operation
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a fictional entity
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a type of structure
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a physical process
  • malformed secondary wikidata tag

wikipedia tag links to 404
Wikipedia article linked from OSM object using wikipedia tag is missing.
Often article was moved without leaving redirect and wikipedia tag should be edited to point to the new one.
Article may be deleted and no longer existing, or link was never valid. In such cases wikipedia tag should be deleted.

should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a transport accident
linked wikidata entry (Q1801568) is about a transport accident, so it is very unlikely to be correct
subject:wikipedia=* tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present
object categorised by Wikidata - wrong classification may be caused by wrong data on Wikidata
Gedenkstein für Opfer des Hubschrauberabsturzes - an affected OSM element that may be improved

overpass query usable in JOSM that will load all objects where this specific error is present:
[out:xml][timeout:3600]; ( node(364753418)['wikidata'='Q1801568']; ); (._;>;); out meta qt;//['should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a transport accident']

should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to an object that exists outside physical reality
linked wikidata entry (Q151761) is about an object that exists outside physical reality, so it is very unlikely to be correct
subject:wikipedia=* tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present

should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to an animal or plant (and not an individual one)
linked wikidata entry (Q176647) is about an animal or plant (and not an individual one), so it is very unlikely to be correct
subject:wikipedia=* tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present
object categorised by Wikidata - wrong classification may be caused by wrong data on Wikidata

linked wikidata entry (Q2604175) is about a chain store, so it is very unlikely to be correct
subject:wikipedia=* or brand:wikipedia tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present
object categorised by Wikidata - wrong classification may be caused by wrong data on Wikidata

wikipedia tag in outdated form and there is mismatch between links
wikipedia tag in outdated form (['wikipedia:nl'], with following conflicts: ['nl:Spoorlijn Gütersloh - Hövelhof gives link Q19838736 conflicting with another link Q16434723']). Mismatch between different links happened and requires human judgment to solve.

wikipedia wikidata mismatch - wikipedia points to disambiguation page and wikidata does not
wikidata and wikipedia tags link to a different objects, because wikidata tag points to a redirect that should be followed
article claims to point to disambig, wikidata does not. wikidata tag is likely to be correct, wikipedia tag almost certainly is not

should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a social movement
linked wikidata entry (Q15303877) is about a social movement, so it is very unlikely to be correct
subject:wikipedia=* tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present
object categorised by Wikidata - wrong classification may be caused by wrong data on Wikidata
an affected OSM element that may be improved
